title: Stunning 4K Drone Footage of City Skyline at Night | Aerial Urban Nightscape #shorts
creator: SkyWave Free Footage (@SkyWavefreefootage)
notes: BLUE-HOUR/NIGHT CITYSCAPE EXEMPLAR (subgenre: blue-hour, sunset-into-night, or astro drone landscape reels) -- added following the same per-record verification rigor as this pack's original entries (see e.g. bbc-earth-aerial.json, lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json). Independently re-verified this session -- not merely trusted from the prior candidate JSON -- via two separate calls to YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/Wo0b00JB2tU&format=json), one via WebFetch and one via a direct curl in this session's shell, both returning identical results: title 'Stunning 4K Drone Footage of City Skyline at Night | Aerial Urban Nightscape #shorts', author_name 'SkyWave Free Footage', author_url 'https://www.youtube.com/@SkyWavefreefootage', and a portrait embed of width=113/height=200 -- this vertical (9:16-derived) embed geometry independently confirms the native /shorts/ URL really is presented as a YouTube Short rather than a landscape upload mislabeled with a /shorts/ path, matching this pack's 'short-form-reel' subgenre criterion on format grounds. Title and author match the prior agent's candidate record exactly, so the source is treated as confirmed in this session rather than inherited on trust. No Creative Commons or other explicit license marking was present in the oEmbed response (oEmbed does not surface CC licensing metadata at all, so this is not evidence either way, but no corroborating CC claim was found elsewhere in this session), so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `license_category` is set to 'all-rights-reserved'. Consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on retaining all-rights-reserved footage. CAVEAT, carried forward from the prior agent's verification and independently plausible given the channel name itself: 'SkyWave Free Footage' brands itself as a free/no-copyright stock-footage aggregator channel (per its handle and the genre of content it posts), so while this specific video and its metadata are genuine and independently reproducible via oEmbed, the footage itself likely originates as licensed/aggregated b-roll rather than being this channel's own original aerial capture -- flagged explicitly here for downstream reviewer judgment about provenance, not resolved by this session (no further verification of the footage's original capture source was attempted). `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field, and no YouTube Data API call or playback timing was performed in this session, so rather than fabricate a plausible-looking Shorts-length number (e.g. assuming ~15-60s) this field stays unset pending a real API call per reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is a stock-footage-aggregator upload, not a formally judged contest or broadcaster showcase entry, and no award or festival selection was found or claimed anywhere in the verified metadata -- inventing one would violate the no-fabrication constraint. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was downloaded or streamed to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.70-0.88 range: sharpness=0.72 (title claims '4K' capture, a positive signal, but this is offset by two negative signals with no positive evidence to counter them -- night footage of a lit skyline is inherently noisier per-pixel than daylight footage at the same nominal resolution due to higher ISO/gain, and the stock-aggregator-repurposing caveat above means this may be a re-encoded, re-compressed copy rather than a true first-generation master, both of which plausibly degrade measured Laplacian-variance sharpness relative to this pack's daylight exemplars); exposure=0.71 (a blue-hour/night skyline is a genuinely hard dynamic-range case -- bright individual lit windows and streetlights against a dark sky and largely unlit foreground -- comparable in difficulty to lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json's sunset backlighting case (scored 0.70), and no HDR/log-profile or bracketed-exposure claim was found in the verified metadata to indicate this was handled better than a typical single-exposure capture); motion_smoothness=0.80 (single continuous 'reveal' shot per the verified title/format, and stock-footage-aggregator b-roll intended for resale/reuse is typically shot deliberately steady -- usable footage requires smooth, gimbal-stabilized motion or it would not be commercially viable as stock -- so this is scored near this pack's higher end, similar to bbc-earth-aerial.json's 0.80, though still short of this pack's very top scores since no explicit stabilization-hardware or flight-mode claim was independently verified in this session). `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.72, exposure=0.71, motion_smoothness=0.80, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (the module's current default, 'default-v2': sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25 each) via `.venv/bin/activate` then `python3 -c "..."` in this session, returning 0.7433333333333333 -- since `composition` is None, `compute_composite_score` skips the zero-information composition slot and renormalizes over only the three populated signals, so the result equals a straight (0.72+0.71+0.80)/3 = 0.7433333... regardless of which named weights profile is passed, confirmed against the function's actual return value. Rounded to 0.7433 here. `editorial_style` reflects a genuine attempt, not a shortcut: the two oEmbed calls made in this session returned only title, author, thumbnail URL, and embed HTML/dimensions -- no cut count, shot-length, transition-style, or pacing description of any kind, and no browser-automation/playback tool (e.g. claude-in-chrome) was invoked in this session to scrub or watch the actual video. A prior agent's editorial-review pass likewise recorded `review_method: not_reviewed` with all quantitative fields null/empty. Although the confirmed vertical embed geometry strongly suggests a genuine short-form-reel edit, `format` is left null here rather than asserted, matching this pack's established convention (e.g. lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json) of not inferring editorial-style fields from platform/URL convention alone without an actual playback or text-provenance review.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Live playback review was not performed in this session: no browser-automation/video playback tool (e.g. claude-in-chrome) was invoked to scrub or watch this Short. Two oEmbed calls (WebFetch and direct curl) against https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/Wo0b00JB2tU&format=json returned only title, author_name/author_url, thumbnail_url, and embed html/width/height (113x200) -- no cut count, shot length, transition style, or pacing description of any kind. The confirmed 113x200 portrait embed geometry is consistent with a single-shot vertical reveal per the verified title ('Stunning 4K Drone Footage of City Skyline at Night'), but this is an inference from embed geometry and title text, not an observation from actual playback, so `format` is left null rather than asserted as 'short-form-reel' without a real review.
